_comment: Per-backend regression pins. Each entry pins (a) the GGUF under test to a specific HF revision SHA so an upstream re-quantise doesn't silently regress us, and (b) the reference-dump archive in cstr/crispasr-regression-fixtures (also pinned). See tests/regression/README.md. Entries with skip_diff=true run transcript-only checks (no ref dump needed yet); see README for the fast-track workflow.

fixtures._note: Pinned. To re-bake, run `tools/kaggle-regression.py` with MODE=rebake UPLOAD=1; the new commit SHA gets pasted here as a reviewable diff.

backends.name: parakeet-tdt-0.6b-ja
backends.source_model_note: HF id passed to NeMo's ASRModel.from_pretrained() by tools/reference_backends/parakeet.py. Only needed for re-bake (Kaggle); the nightly validate path doesn't touch source weights.
backends._sample_note: Sample WAVs live in the fixtures HF repo rather than the source tree; samples/.gitignore excludes them by default to keep the repo small.
backends.expected_transcript: ピッチャーの岡本は1回戦の鳥取城北戦は8回、6対33点リードの場面で3人目でマウンドに上がりました。
backends.expected_transcript_source: captured on commit 3a9b294c (post-issue-#88 fix) with HF revision fd1963a, M1 Metal CPU+BLAS, decode_temperature=0. NOT the human ground-truth (ground_truth.srt in the fixtures repo, which reads 山本 / Yamamoto rather than the model's ピッチャーの岡本 / Okamoto). Lock-in is for regression, not correctness.
backends.diff_thresholds_note: mel_spectrogram tightened from 0.95 to 0.999 after drop_last_frame fix (commit 07cfcff). NeMo ref needs rebake to clip mel to feat_len; current ref has one extra frame that the comparison truncates.

backends.name: moonshine-tiny
backends.source_model_note: HF id for tools/reference_backends/moonshine.py. Only needed for re-bake.

gguf._file_note: F16 GGUF used for diff regression (cos=1.000 vs PyTorch). The q4_k variant uses Q4_0 for attn weights, giving cos_min~0.79 — not suitable for diff. Transcript is identical between F16 and Q4K.

backends.expected_transcript: And so my fellow Americans ask not what your country can do for you, ask what you can do for your country.
backends.expected_transcript_source: captured 2026-05-15 with HF revision 28281de, CPU-only, samples/jfk.wav (11s JFK speech)
backends.diff_thresholds_note: F16 GGUF vs PyTorch reference gives cos_min=1.000. Threshold set at 0.999 to absorb any future F16 rounding.

backends.name: moonshine-base
backends.source_model_note: HF id for tools/reference_backends/moonshine.py. Only needed for re-bake.

gguf._file_note: F16 GGUF used for diff regression (cos=1.000 vs PyTorch). The q4_k variant uses Q4_0 for attn weights, giving cos_min~0.83 — not suitable for diff.

backends.expected_transcript: And so my fellow Americans ask not what your country can do for you ask what you can do for your country
backends.expected_transcript_source: captured 2026-05-15 with HF revision bfb1ef9, CPU-only, samples/jfk.wav (moonshine-base F16 greedy, no comma or trailing period — model-deterministic)
backends.diff_thresholds_note: F16 GGUF vs PyTorch reference gives cos_min=1.000. Threshold 0.999 for headroom.

backends.name: moonshine-streaming-tiny

gguf._file_note: F32 GGUF used for diff regression (cos_min=1.000 vs GGUF-direct reference). The q4_k variant is also available for transcript testing.

backends.expected_transcript: ANd so, my fellow Americans, ask not what your country can do for you, ask what you can do for your country..
backends.expected_transcript_source: captured 2026-05-15 with HF revision 56e0ac3, CPU-only, samples/jfk.wav. Note: leading 'ANd' capitalisation and trailing '..' are model-deterministic output, not a bug.
backends.diff_thresholds_note: F32 GGUF vs GGUF-direct Python reference gives cos_min=1.000. Threshold 0.999 for headroom.

backends.name: parakeet-tdt-0.6b-en
backends._skip_diff_note: Q4K model diverges from F16/F32 NeMo reference (all encoder layers cos_min<0.9). The V3 reference needs an F16 GGUF; q4_k is too coarse for diff.
backends.expected_transcript: And so, my fellow Americans, ask not what your country can do for you, ask what you can do for your country.
backends.expected_transcript_source: captured 2026-05-15 with HF revision 815cc1b, CPU-only, samples/jfk.wav

gguf._file_note: F16 GGUF used for diff regression (cos_min=0.999280 vs NeMo). The q4_k variant diverges too much for diff.

backends.expected_transcript: And so, my fellow Americans, ask not what your country can do for you, ask what you can do for your country.
backends.expected_transcript_source: captured 2026-05-17 with HF revision b3715a5, CPU-only, samples/jfk.wav
backends.diff_thresholds_note: F16 GGUF vs NeMo reference with dither=0 and drop_last_frame. mel cos=1.0, encoder cos=0.999280. Layers 18-19 are F16 near-misses (0.9977/0.9988).

gguf._file_note: F32 GGUF used for diff regression (cos_min=0.999515). The q4_k variant diverges too much.

backends.expected_transcript: AND SO MY FELLOW AMERICANS ASK NOT WHAT YOUR COUNTRY CAN DO FOR YOU, ASK WHAT YOU CAN DO FOR YOUR COUNTRY.
backends.expected_transcript_source: captured 2026-05-15 with HF revision 076633a, CPU-only, samples/jfk.wav

backends.name: glm-asr-nano
backends.source_model_note: HF id for tools/reference_backends/glm_asr.py. Only needed for re-bake.

gguf._file_note: F32 GGUF used for diff regression (cos_min=1.000). The q4_k/q8_0 variants diverge.

backends.expected_transcript: And so, my fellow Americans, ask not what your country can do for you. Ask what you can do for your country.
backends.expected_transcript_source: captured 2026-05-15 with HF revision 0eed3da, CPU-only, samples/jfk.wav
backends.transcript_tolerance_note: glm-asr-nano's LLM-style decoder ties on score-boundary punctuation/case ('you. Ask' vs 'you, ask') — the encoder+mel checks BOTH pass at cos=1.000 (model weights are identical) but a runner/library upgrade can nudge a softmax score by 1e-7 and flip a decode tie. 2% CER / 5% WER catches real regressions (missing/wrong words, language confusion) while tolerating cosmetic decode drift. Other backends keep the tighter byte-equal bar by omitting this field.
backends.diff_thresholds_note: F32 GGUF vs Python reference gives cos_min=1.000 for both mel and encoder.

backends.name: cohere-transcribe
backends._skip_diff_note: mel passes (cos=0.999638) after transpose + drop_last_frame fix, but encoder_output fails (cos_min=-0.253) — 48-layer conformer diverges, needs investigation.
backends._skip_preflight_note: cstr/cohere-transcribe-GGUF is private and HF account has hit private-repo storage limit (403). Remove skip_preflight once repo is made public or space freed.
backends.expected_transcript: And so, my fellow Americans, ask not what your country can do for you, ask what you can do for your country.
backends.expected_transcript_source: captured 2026-05-15 with HF revision b9bcdb1, CPU-only, samples/jfk.wav

backends.name: granite-speech-4.1-2b
backends._skip_diff_note: mel passes (cos=0.999997) but encoder diverges (cos_min=-0.077, all 16 conformer layers fail). Needs per-layer investigation. Granite NLE outputs lowercase without punctuation.
backends.expected_transcript: and so my fellow americans ask not what your country can do for you ask what you can do for your country
backends.expected_transcript_source: captured 2026-05-15 with HF revision 7ee888f, CPU-only, samples/jfk.wav. IBM Granite NLE outputs normalised lowercase without punctuation.
